name: Roll overdue Notion tasks forward and flag ones rolled too often

nodes.name: Roll Overdue Dates and Flag Stale

parameters.content:
## Roll overdue Notion tasks forward and flag ones rolled too often

### How it works

1. On a daily schedule, it reads every row from the Notion database you choose.
2. A Code node finds rows whose due date is in the past and whose status is not done.
3. Each overdue task has its due date rolled forward to today, or the next business day.
4. A rolled counter is incremented, and a Stale flag is set once a task has been rolled past a threshold.
5. Only the rows that changed are written back in place, so finished and not-yet-due tasks are never touched.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Connect a Notion credential and share the target database with the integration.
- [ ] In Get Open Tasks, select the database that holds your tasks.
- [ ] In Roll Overdue Dates and Flag Stale, set the property names, DONE_VALUES, STALE_THRESHOLD, and ROLL_TO at the top of the code.
- [ ] In Update Rolled Tasks, map the Due, Rolled, and Stale property values to your own property names.

### Customization

Set ROLL_TO to today or nextBusinessDay, change STALE_THRESHOLD to control how many rolls make a task stale, and adjust Every Morning at 7am to run on any schedule you prefer.

## Read open tasks on a schedule

Runs each morning and pulls every row from the database with full property data.

## Find overdue tasks and roll them

Skips finished and not-yet-due rows, rolls each overdue task forward, and flags the ones rolled too often.

## Write the rolled tasks back

Updates only the rows that changed: the new due date, the incremented counter, and the Stale flag.

main.node: Roll Overdue Dates and Flag Stale
